BEADS WORKFLOW — MANDATORY RULES (violation = wasted tokens/duplicate work for user):
1. BEFORE any work: bd update <id> --claim --status in_progress
2. When a new issue is found mid-work: bd create --discovered-from <current-id> IMMEDIATELY — not in conversation, not later
3. BEFORE any commit: run bd list --status=in_progress, close every bead whose scope is done, narrow unfinished ones with exact remaining scope — THEN commit
4. Commit order: close bead → commit → never the reverse
5. Verification beads have no commit — must be closed in the same pass they pass, or left open with exact failure note
6. SESSION END: close all done beads, narrow all open ones, commit, git push, bd dolt push
7. An untouched-looking open bead WILL be repeated by the next agent. That costs the user real money.

Browser automation MCP guide: (1) Vercel MCP (mcp__0bd10557) — use for all Vercel dashboard data: logs, deployments, speed insights, runtime cache. Already authenticated. No browser needed. (2) Playwright MCP (mcp__Claude_Preview__) — use for PT Tracker E2E testing, cross-browser verification. Lowest token cost. (3) Chrome DevTools MCP — use for performance traces, network inspection, console debug, Core Web Vitals. Token cost ~19k. (4) Claude in Chrome — backup only when others fail, conflicts with user extensions. Rule: Vercel data = Vercel MCP; PT Tracker testing = Playwright; performance/network debug = Chrome DevTools; Claude in Chrome = last resort only.

Browser automation MCP selection guide: (1) Vercel MCP (mcp__0bd10557) — use for all Vercel dashboard data: logs, deployments, runtime cache, speed insights. Already authenticated. No browser needed. (2) Playwright MCP — use for PT Tracker E2E testing, cross-browser verification, headless use. Lowest token cost ~13.7k. Accessibility-tree selectors are reliable. Installed in mcp.json via npx @playwright/mcp@latest --headless. (3) Chrome DevTools MCP — use for performance traces, network request inspection, console debugging, Core Web Vitals. Token cost ~19k but best for deep debugging. (4) Claude in Chrome — backup only, use when other tools fail. Extensions may cause issues. Rule: Vercel dashboard = Vercel MCP; PT Tracker testing = Playwright MCP; performance/network debug = Chrome DevTools MCP; Claude in Chrome = last resort backup.
